note: ~650 statute miles / ~1,046 km. Doubled once you dip under the Peloponnese before the Corinth Canal (1893).
At ~5 knots and up to ~100 nm/day, Troy to Ithaca is 6 days to two weeks.
A modern superyacht does the direct trip in about a week.
A cruise ship covers it in under 24 hours.
Logged 'several thousand sea miles,' wintering in Malta — vs Odysseus's ten years.
3-4 months Troy to Ithaca in a 54-ft replica Bronze Age galley, Argo.
~4-6 knots average for a ~55-ft black-pitched war-galley with a single square sail.
Up to ~100 nm/day running before the wind.
A galley driven under sail = 630-900 miles in 9 days, hence a Tunisia landfall.
A crew holding position drifts only ~30 miles/day, so 9 days = ~270 miles, landing in Libya.
